title: CrossFire UCC File Export-Import (.ucc)
description:
CrossFire UCC File Export-Import Add-on for Blender

Transform your CrossFire map creation workflow with this powerful Blender add-on, designed specifically for CrossFire Map Workshop developers.
This comprehensive tool enables seamless import and export of UCC (Unreal Coordinate Container) files, bridging the gap between Blender’s 3D modeling power and CrossFire’s engine requirements.

🎯 Precise UCC File Handling

  • Import UCC files with full preservation of coordinates, rotations, and scale

  • Export Blender scenes to UCC with advanced transformation options

  • Built-in data validation prevents corruption and ensures compatibility

🔧 Advanced Transformation Controls

  • Z-axis rotation adjustment for global scene orientation

  • Position multipliers for coordinate system conversion

  • Scale multipliers with automatic negative-to-positive conversion

  • Position offsets for precise map alignment

⚡ Streamlined Workflow

  • Quick Export Test for rapid iteration

  • Temporary scene handling to keep your original work safe

  • Detailed logging system to track export operations

  • Batch processing for multiple objects and large maps

🎮 Game-Ready Output

  • Optimized for CrossFire Map Workshop integration

  • Preserves object naming conventions

  • Maintains transformation hierarchy

  • Fully compatible with existing CrossFire workflows

💼 Perfect For

  • Game level designers building for CrossFire

  • Map creators using Blender for assets and layouts

  • Developers needing accurate coordinate conversion

  • Teams requiring standardized, reliable export processes

This add-on eliminates tedious manual conversion, letting you focus on designing exceptional environments.
Whether you’re crafting competitive arenas, custom game modes, or detailed architectural spaces, this tool ensures your Blender creations translate perfectly into the CrossFire engine.

Installation is simple, with an intuitive N-Panel interface that integrates seamlessly into Blender. Comprehensive logging and error handling provide reliability and confidence in your workflow.

🚀 Take your CrossFire map creation to the next level with professional-grade import/export capabilities that guarantee precision, performance, and ease of use.
documentation:
CrossFire UCC File Export-Import Add-on Documentation


=================================================================


TABLE OF CONTENTS

1. Introduction

2. Installation

3. User Interface

4. Import Functions

5. Export Functions

6. Advanced Settings

7. Workflow Examples

8. Troubleshooting

9. Technical Notes


=================================================================


1. INTRODUCTION


The CrossFire UCC File Export-Import add-on is a specialized tool for Blender that enables seamless integration between Blender's 3D environment and CrossFire Map Workshop. UCC (Unreal Coordinate Container) files contain spatial data for game objects including position, rotation, scale, and naming information.


This add-on is designed for:

- Game level designers

- Map creators for CrossFire

- Developers working with coordinate conversion

- Teams requiring precise export workflows


=================================================================


2. INSTALLATION


Requirements:

- Blender 3.0.0 or later

- Windows operating system (recommended)


Installation Steps:

1. Download the add-on zip file

2. Open Blender and go to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons

3. Click "Install..." and select the zip file

4. Enable the "CrossFire UCC File Export-Import" add-on

5. The panel will appear in the 3D Viewport sidebar under "CF Tools"


=================================================================


3. USER INTERFACE


The add-on interface is located in:

View3D > Sidebar > CF Tools > UCC File Operations


Main Controls:

- Z-axis Rotation Angle: Global rotation applied during export (-360° to 360°)

- Position Multiplier: Coordinate scaling factor (0.1 to 1000.0)

- Position Offset: X, Y, Z offset values (-1000.0 to 1000.0)

- Scale Multiplier: Size scaling factor (0.1 to 1000.0)

- Negative to Positive: Auto-convert negative scale values

- Source File: Choose between Quick (mf5) or Standard (mf6) templates


Action Buttons:

- Import UCC: Import UCC file into Blender

- Export UCC: Export scene to UCC file

- Quick Export Test: Fast export to predefined location


=================================================================


4. IMPORT FUNCTIONS


Import UCC Files:

1. Click "Import UCC"

2. Navigate to your UCC file

3. Select the file and click "Import UCC"


Import Process:

- Reads object coordinates, rotations, and scale data

- Creates a text block with imported data

- Validates data integrity (checks for NaN/infinite values)

- Reports number of successfully imported objects


Data Format in Text Block:

Name: [ObjectName], Location: (x,y,z), Rotation: (x,y,z,w), Size: (x,y,z), 

Coord Index: [position], Rotation Index: [position], Size Index: [position]


=================================================================


5. EXPORT FUNCTIONS


Standard Export:

1. Set up your scene with mesh objects

2. Configure export settings in the panel

3. Click "Export UCC"

4. Choose save location and filename

5. Review the export log for details


Quick Export Test:

1. Configure your scene

2. Select source template (mf5 or mf6)

3. Click "Quick Export Test"

4. File exports to ~/Documents/CFSystem/UCC/untitled.ucc


Export Process:

- Creates temporary scene for processing

- Applies global Z-axis rotation around combined center

- Converts coordinate systems (Blender to UCC)

- Applies position and scale multipliers

- Handles negative scale conversion

- Clears unused object slots

- Generates detailed operation log


=================================================================


6. ADVANCED SETTINGS


Z-axis Rotation Angle:

- Applied globally around the combined center of all objects

- Useful for coordinate system alignment

- Default: 90 degrees


Position Multiplier:

- Scales coordinate values during export

- Blender units to UCC units conversion

- Default: 1.0


Position Offset:

- Adds offset to object positions

- Separate controls for X, Y, Z axes

- Useful for map alignment


Scale Multiplier:

- Scales object sizes during export

- Applied to all scale dimensions

- Default: 1.0


Negative to Positive Conversion:

- Automatically converts negative scale values to positive

- Handles mirrored objects correctly

- Logs which axes were converted


Source File Selection:

- mf5 (Quick): Streamlined template

- mf6 (Standard): Full feature template

- Templates stored in Core/ folder


=================================================================


7. WORKFLOW EXAMPLES


Basic Export Workflow:

1. Create or import 3D models in Blender

2. Position objects as desired

3. Set objects to use Quaternion rotation mode

4. Configure export settings

5. Use Quick Export Test for iteration

6. Use standard Export for final output


Coordinate System Conversion:

- Blender uses right-handed coordinates

- UCC format uses specific scaling (35x multiplier)

- Z-axis rotation handles orientation differences

- Position multiplier adjusts coordinate scale


Multi-Object Scenes:

- Objects are processed in alphabetical order by name

- Each object's transform data is preserved

- Unused slots are automatically zeroed

- Maximum ~10,000 objects supported


=================================================================


8. TROUBLESHOOTING


Common Issues:


"Source file not found":

- Ensure Core/mf5.ucc and Core/mf6.ucc exist

- Check file permissions

- Verify add-on installation


"Object exceeds file size":

- Too many objects for template capacity

- Reduce object count or use larger template


"Cannot create export directory":

- Check folder permissions

- Ensure sufficient disk space

- Verify path accessibility


Rotation Issues:

- Ensure objects use Quaternion rotation mode

- Add-on automatically converts rotation modes

- Check rotation order in complex hierarchies


Scale Problems:

- Enable "Negative to Positive" for mirrored objects

- Adjust Scale Multiplier for size conversion

- Check for zero or infinite scale values


=================================================================


9. TECHNICAL NOTES


File Format Details:

- UCC files use binary format

- Fixed 200-byte blocks per object

- Coordinate data: 12 bytes (3 floats)

- Rotation data: 16 bytes (4 floats, quaternion)

- Scale data: 12 bytes (3 floats)


Coordinate Conversion:

UCC_X = Blender_X * 35 * Position_Multiplier

UCC_Y = Blender_Y * 35 * Position_Multiplier

UCC_Z = Blender_Z * 35 * Position_Multiplier


Memory Management:

- Temporary scenes are automatically cleaned up

- Original scene is preserved during export

- Text blocks store operation logs


Performance Considerations:

- Large scenes (>1000 objects) may take time to process

- Quick Export Test is optimized for iteration

- Logging can be disabled for better performance


Data Validation:

- Checks for NaN and infinite values

- Validates file boundaries

- Reports data integrity issues


=================================================================


For support and updates, visit the CrossFire development community.

This documentation covers version 2.0.0 of the add-on.
